# @stll/aho-corasick

> NAPI-RS bindings to the Rust aho-corasick crate
> for Node.js and Bun. Multi-pattern string
> searching in linear time.

## Quick start

```typescript
import { AhoCorasick } from "@stll/aho-corasick";

const ac = new AhoCorasick(["foo", "bar", "baz"]);

ac.isMatch("hello foo world"); // true

ac.findIter("foo bar baz");
// [
//   { pattern: 0, start: 0, end: 3 },
//   { pattern: 1, start: 4, end: 7 },
//   { pattern: 2, start: 8, end: 11 },
// ]

ac.replaceAll("foo bar baz", ["A", "B", "C"]);
// "A B C"
```

## API

### AhoCorasick

Constructor: `new AhoCorasick(patterns: string[], options?: Options)`

Options:
- `matchKind`: `"leftmost-first"` (default) or `"leftmost-longest"`
- `caseInsensitive`: `boolean` (default `false`, ASCII-only: A-Z to a-z)
- `dfa`: `boolean` (default `false`, force DFA mode)
- `wholeWords`: `boolean` (default `false`, Unicode-aware word boundary detection; CJK always passes)

Methods:
- `findIter(haystack: string): Match[]` — non-overlapping matches
- `findOverlappingIter(haystack: string): Match[]` — all overlapping matches
- `isMatch(haystack: string): boolean` — any pattern matches?
- `replaceAll(haystack: string, replacements: string[]): string` — replace; `replacements[i]` replaces pattern `i`
- `patternCount: number` — getter, number of patterns

### StreamMatcher

Constructor: `new StreamMatcher(patterns: string[], options?: Options)`

For searching across chunked/streamed input. Handles cross-boundary matches automatically.

Methods:
- `write(chunk: Buffer): Match[]` — feed chunk, returns matches with global byte offsets
- `flush(): Match[]` — finalize after last chunk
- `reset(): void` — reset for reuse

### Types

```typescript
type Match = {
  pattern: number; // index into patterns array
  start: number;   // UTF-16 code unit offset
  end: number;     // exclusive
  text: string;    // the matched substring
};
```

Offsets are UTF-16 code units, compatible with `String.prototype.slice()`. `text` is `haystack.slice(start, end)`.

## Important notes

- Case insensitivity is ASCII-only (A-Z to a-z). Turkish İ, German ß, and other Unicode case folding is not supported.
- `leftmost-first`: pattern insertion order determines which pattern wins at a position. `leftmost-longest`: longest match wins regardless of order.
- The automaton is built once at construction and reused. Construction is O(patterns); search is O(haystack + matches).
- `replaceAll` requires exactly `patternCount` replacements or it throws.
- Native binary for Node.js/Bun; WASM fallback for browsers. Bundlers auto-select via the `browser` field.
